title: Angraecum serpens
taxonomy: Angraecum serpens is a species of orchid, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This genus is a part of the tribe Vandeae, which is a subtribe of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The tribe Vandeae is further classified under the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. The subfamily Epidendroideae is known for its diverse range of species, many of which are epiphytic. Angraecum serpens is a unique species within this diverse group, characterized by its climbing habit and distinctive flowers.
uses: Angraecum serpens, a species of orchid native to tropical Africa, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
